title: Business Model Design
use_when: Design, compare, or document business models using the right canvas for the situation. Use when a founder, product team, strategist, or innovation team asks to fill a business model canvas, lean canvas, platform canvas, value creation and capture model, revenue/cost logic, customer/user/payer model, or variants of "what is the business model", "how does this make money", "which canvas should I use", "turn this idea into a business model", or "compare these business models".
do_not_use_when: Do not use Business Model Design when the request is mainly a different lifecycle artifact; route to the narrower Productize skill that owns that artifact instead. Do not use it when the user lacks enough context to distinguish facts, assumptions, and decision risk.
output_artifact: Business Model Design business-model memo with value capture logic, risks, and next tests
framework_fit: Business Model Design fits Business Model work in the Strategize lifecycle when the user needs Business Model Design business-model memo with value capture logic, risks, and next tests and has enough product context to make tradeoffs explicit. Use it to produce a decision-ready artifact, not a framework tour.
failure_modes: Produces Business Model Design business-model memo with value capture logic, risks, and next tests without tying recommendations to the user's Strategize stage, evidence, and decision pressure.
Treats Business Model Design as generic Business Model advice instead of naming assumptions, missing inputs, risks, and the next validation step.
Fails to route away from Business Model Design when another Productize skill owns the requested artifact more directly.

examples.prompt: Use $business-model-design to turn this business context into a decision-ready Business Model Design business-model memo with value capture logic, risks, and next tests.
examples.expected_artifact: Business Model Design business-model memo with value capture logic, risks, and next tests
